description: Port Blair, Havelock, and Neil Island together offer the perfect Andaman island experience. Explore Port Blair’s historic sites and coastal views, enjoy Havelock’s world-famous white-sand beaches and water sports, and relax on Neil Island’s serene beaches and coral reefs. A beautiful blend of adventure, nature, and tranquility.
cost_includes:
3 star premium hotel stay 
 All transfers and sightseeing by AC Vehicle. 
 this cost valid for 6 adult traveling together 
 Breakfast, Dinner.   
 Toll Tax. 
 Parking. 
 Driver Allowance.

 All cost from EX Destination

Any meals other than those mentioned above inclusion

 All monument entry Fees  
 Any portage at airports and hotels, tips, insurance, wine, mineral water, telephone charges, and all  
Items of personal nature. 
 Any services not specifically mentioned in the inclusions. 
 Entrance fees to Monuments & other sight-seeing points. 
 Expenses caused by factors beyond our control like rail and flight delays, roadblocks, vehicle         malfunctions, political disturbances etc.                    
 Anything Not mentioned in inclusions.

faqs.title: 1. What is the best time to visit Port Blair, Havelock, and Neil Island?
faqs.content: The best time is October to May, when the weather is calm, sunny, and perfect for beaches and water activities.

faqs.title: 2. How many days are ideal for this trip?
faqs.content: A comfortable trip usually takes 5 to 7 days to enjoy all three islands without rushing.

faqs.title: 3. What are the must-visit attractions during this tour?
faqs.content:
Port Blair: Cellular Jail, Corbyn’s Cove, Ross Island

Havelock: Radhanagar Beach, Elephant Beach, water sports

Neil Island: Natural Bridge, Bharatpur Beach, Laxmanpur Beach

faqs.title: 4. How do I travel between the islands?
faqs.content: Travel is by private ferries like Makruzz / Nautika or government ferries. Advance booking is recommended during peak season.

faqs.title: 5. What should I pack for an Andaman trip?
faqs.content: Carry light beachwear, sunscreen, sunglasses, hats, slippers, waterproof bags, and for snorkeling trips, a spare set of clothes. Comfortable shoes are useful for sightseeing.
